_meta.dataset: DemoTrack — curated label discovery database (seed: 17 labels)
_meta.selection_rule: Every label clears the >=3-of-5 quality bar: Beatport chart presence, booking impact, streaming credibility, scene respect, accessible to unknown artists.
_meta.verification_rule: Submission details verified against the label's own page where available, cross-checked with a second source. Provenance + fetch date recorded per record. Unverified/single-source fields flagged via confidence.

field_notes.tier: ELITE | A | B (prestige axis, from spec)
field_notes.access_path_verified: what current public evidence actually shows; differs from spec where a public portal exists
field_notes.submission.method: email | form | dm | open-window | none-public | unknown
field_notes.confidence: high (official page or 2 independent sources) | medium (single secondary source) | low (unverified / no channel found)

labels.name: Drumcode
labels.why_it_belongs: Scene-defining techno institution (est. 1996). A long game, not a cold target.

submission.notes: No public demo channel; managed by Adam Beyer. Reach via relationship/established A&R only.

labels.name: Experts Only
labels.why_it_belongs: John Summit's label — peak of the current scene.

requirements: Submit via the Trackstack portal linked from expertsonlyrecs.com
submission.notes: DISCREPANCY: spec lists needs-warm-intro, but a public 'Send Demo' Trackstack portal is live on the official site — effectively cold-demo-friendly.

labels.name: Black Book
labels.why_it_belongs: Chris Lake's label — a Beatport #1 machine.

requirements: Send up to 5 tracks via the on-site Trackstack portal
submission.notes: DISCREPANCY: spec lists needs-warm-intro, but a public demo-submission portal (Trackstack) is live on blackbookrecs.com.

labels.name: Catch & Release
labels.why_it_belongs: Fisher's label — massive reach and chart dominance.

requirements: Send up to 5 tracks via the Trackstack portal on followthefishtv.com (the DEMOS page)
submission.notes: DISCREPANCY: spec lists needs-warm-intro, but a public Trackstack demo portal is live.

labels.name: Repopulate Mars
labels.why_it_belongs: Where Mau P broke through. Openly accepts demos.

requirements: Private SoundCloud or Dropbox links ONLY (no Google Drive / WeTransfer)
Max 4 demos at once; use a private playlist for multiple
Turn downloads ON
Prefer extended/full mixes over radio edits
submission.notes: One submission covers Repopulate Mars, South Of Saturn, and North Of Neptune.

labels.name: Dirtybird
labels.why_it_belongs: Fun, quirky lane; a known career-launcher.

requirements: Upload via the Label-Worx 'DIRTYBIRD DemoBox' page
No feedback offered on all demos; they contact you if signing
submission.notes: Official demo channel is the Label-Worx DemoBox portal.

labels.name: Hellbent
labels.why_it_belongs: Cloonee's label — built for unknowns; charts constantly.

submission.notes: NEEDS VERIFICATION: no public demo portal found on hellbentrecs.com (a Shopify store) as of 2026-06-01. Past 'first release' contests referenced on Reddit. Check Cloonee's socials / a LabelRadar or Trackstack portal before recording a channel.

labels.name: Three Six Zero
labels.why_it_belongs: Open submissions; growing fast (Dom Dolla's home).

requirements: Submit via the LabelRadar portal
submission.notes: General/management contacts info@threesixzero.com and hi@threesixzero.com exist but are NOT demo channels — use the LabelRadar portal for demos.

labels.name: Hot Creations
labels.why_it_belongs: Jamie Jones's label — deep scene credibility.

requirements: Historically: send a private SoundCloud link to the 'hotcreationsdemos' account
submission.notes: NEEDS RE-VERIFICATION of current policy — single source (the public 'hotcreationsdemos' SoundCloud account). Confirm before relying on it.
Email confirmed by two independent directories (labelsbase + findmylabels); not yet cross-checked against the label's own site.

labels.name: Sola
labels.why_it_belongs: Solardo's label (Manchester, UK) — consistent charting; email demos welcome.

submission.notes: Single secondary source (findmylabels) for the email; verify against an official channel before bulk use.

labels.name: Toolroom
labels.why_it_belongs: Solid distribution and a long track record (Mark Knight).

requirements: Upload via the on-site portal (powered by LabelRadar)
They listen to everything but cannot reply to each; contact you if interested
submission.notes: Role emails on the contact page (events/licensing/PR/store) are NOT demo channels.

labels.name: Defected
labels.why_it_belongs: Huge catalogue; one submission effectively covers ~11 sub-labels.

requirements: Streamable links only
WAV format only
Do NOT include artwork or promo materials
Submissions not following the guidelines are auto-deleted
submission.notes: A LabelRadar portal also exists, but the official site directs demos to email.

labels.name: Knee Deep In Sound
labels.why_it_belongs: Hot Since 82's label — a less-competitive melodic lane.

submission.notes: Licensing/management is craig@anglomanagement.co.uk — demos go to demos@hotsince82.com.

labels.name: Suara
labels.why_it_belongs: Coyu's label — darker tech house niche.

requirements: Private SoundCloud links only
No sendspace / yousendit / wetransfer links
submission.notes: From the label's official Facebook; re-verify as the post is not recent.

labels.name: Club Sweat
labels.why_it_belongs: Sweat It Out's house imprint — Australian reach; good for emerging artists.

requirements: Submit via the Sweat It Out DropTrack portal
submission.notes: Portal covers Sweat It Out releases including Club Sweat.

labels.name: Space Yacht
labels.why_it_belongs: Reviews demos live on Twitch — the most transparent route in the scene.

requirements: Submit via the LabelRadar portal
Also: tracks reviewed live on twitch.tv/spaceyacht and on the 'Tune Reactor' on-air reaction series
submission.notes: Both an always-open LabelRadar portal and the live Twitch review window exist.
